claims.claim: Masked execution preserves output equality vs the plain reference for the tested GPT-2 model-level wrapper (full forward + prefill + decode_step + greedy generation).
claims.paper_safe_wording: We empirically verify that the GPT-2 model-level masked wrapper reproduces the plain reference output token-for-token in our tested configurations.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Masked execution provably preserves output equality.
claims.notes: Empirical allclose on tested configs only; no proof.

claims.claim: Masked execution preserves output equality vs the plain reference for the tested modern-decoder model-level wrapper (RMSNorm + SwiGLU + RoPE + GQA, prefill + decode_step + greedy generation).
claims.paper_safe_wording: We empirically verify that the modern decoder-only model-level masked wrapper reproduces the plain reference output token-for-token in our tested configurations.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Masked execution provably preserves output equality for all modern decoder models.
claims.notes: Synthetic and small HF configurations only.

claims.claim: Compatible nonlinear islands preserve output correctness across the tested architectures (decoder-only / encoder-only / encoder-decoder, plus modern decoder).
claims.paper_safe_wording: Compatible mask families (mean-preserving orthogonal for LayerNorm, channel permutation for GELU/ReLU, paired permutation for SwiGLU) preserve output correctness in our tests; we do not claim they extend beyond the tested mask families.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Compatible mask families preserve correctness universally.
claims.notes: Only tested mask families and tested architectures.

claims.claim: KV cache append invariant holds for the tested decoder-only generation paths.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We verify the KV cache append invariant matches the plain reference in our tested decoder-only generation paths.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our KV cache implementation is provably secure.

claims.claim: LoRA masked forward, masked backward, and rank-padded forward/backward reproduce the plain rank-r reference to float64 precision in synthetic single-linear tests.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We empirically verify that the masked LoRA forward / backward / rank-padded primitives match the plain rank-r reference to float64 precision on synthetic single-linear tests.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our LoRA implementation provably matches the plain reference.
claims.notes: Synthetic tile only; no full Qwen / TinyLlama fine-tune.

claims.claim: Multi-layer rank-padded LoRA training reproduces the plain reference loss / gradient / SGD / AdamW update across every per-module slice in synthetic multi-layer tests.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We empirically verify that a synthetic multi-layer rank-padded LoRA training step matches the plain reference on every per-module slice.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our multi-layer LoRA training scheme is identical to plain LoRA for any model.

claims.claim: All Stage 7.4 stronger dummy distributions preserve A_pad B_pad = A_real B_real exactly (cancellation strategies) or with a tracked trusted-side correction (noise_injected_cancellation_dummy), and all five preserve forward / backward / SGD / AdamW update correctness to float64 precision.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Every tested stronger-dummy strategy preserves the rank-padded LoRA invariant A_pad B_pad = A_real B_real (either exactly or via a tracked trusted-side correction) and matches plain training to float64 precision in our tests.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our stronger dummy distributions provably hide the true LoRA rank.

claims.claim: Constant-time training mode (proxy_equalized) reduces the cost-model timing classifier accuracy to near random chance in our tests.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Under the cost-model proxy, equalizing per-step latency to the upper bucket reduces the worst-case timing classifier accuracy to near random chance in our tests.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Constant-time training closes timing side channels.
claims.notes: Cost-model proxy only; no real wall-time, no real side-channel evaluation.

claims.claim: Activation-recovery / linkability adaptive proxy attackers are bounded under the full mitigation bundle (fresh permutation + dense sandwich + boundary pad) in the tested configurations.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Under our adaptive proxy attackers (ridge, small MLP, signature / Sinkhorn permutation recovery, linkability), the full mitigation bundle keeps the worst-case attacker close to random chance in our tested configurations.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our mitigation bundle is secure against arbitrary adversaries.
claims.notes: Proxy attackers only; the artifacts explicitly say `proxy-evaluated, not formal`.

claims.claim: Cost-model timing side-channel proxy is bounded under proxy_equalized constant-time decode / training modes in our tests.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Under our cost-model timing proxy, equalizing latency to the upper bucket reduces classifier accuracy near random chance.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Constant-time mode prevents timing side-channel attacks.
claims.notes: Cost-model proxy only; no real wall-time.

claims.claim: LoRA adapter extraction / gradient leakage proxy attackers are bounded under fresh masks + pad in the tested configurations.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Under our LoRA adapter / gradient leakage proxy attackers, fresh masks + pad bring the linkability AUC close to 0.5 (random chance) in our tests.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our LoRA path is secure against adapter / gradient extraction.
claims.notes: Proxy only; rank still visible from shape under Stage 7.1 (Stage 7.2 addresses shape).

claims.claim: Rank padding hides true_rank from the GPU-visible tensor shape, and stronger dummy distributions reduce the proxy spectral-cliff inference accuracy of true_rank.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Rank padding hides true_rank from tensor shape; under our spectral-cliff / energy / elbow / ensemble proxy detectors, stronger dummy strategies fail to recover true_rank reliably in our tests (`needs_more_evaluation`).
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our rank-padding scheme cryptographically hides the LoRA rank.

claims.claim: Cross-layer adapter linkage is low under fresh masks + paired cancellation across the tested multi-layer configuration.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Under our cross-layer linkability proxy, fresh masks per module keep the AUC near 0.5 across the tested multi-layer configuration.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Cross-layer linkability is impossible.
claims.notes: Proxy only; `fixed_masks_shared_u` baseline is `high` by construction.

claims.claim: Formal / cryptographic / semantic security of the masked path.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We make no formal / cryptographic / semantic security claims.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our scheme provides formal / cryptographic / semantic security.

claims.claim: Real TEE wall-time / hardware-isolation evaluation.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We report measured local-emulation latencies; we do not claim real TEE wall-time.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our wrapper achieves X ms latency on real TEE.

claims.claim: Hardware side-channel security (cache / power / EM).
claims.paper_safe_wording: We do not evaluate hardware side-channels (cache / power / EM).
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our system is resistant to hardware side-channel attacks.
claims.notes: Out of scope; only cost-model timing proxy is evaluated.

claims.claim: Full Qwen / TinyLlama / LLaMA LoRA fine-tuning.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Our LoRA experiments use synthetic single-linear and multi-layer tiles; we do not run full Qwen / TinyLlama / LLaMA LoRA fine-tuning.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our LoRA path supports full Qwen / TinyLlama / LLaMA fine-tuning.

claims.claim: PEFT / DeepSpeed / vLLM / FlashAttention compatibility.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We do not integrate PEFT / DeepSpeed / vLLM / FlashAttention; our LoRA primitives are stand-alone functional API.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our scheme drops into PEFT / DeepSpeed / vLLM / FlashAttention.

claims.claim: padded_rank is hidden from the GPU.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Stage 7.2 / 7.3 / 7.4 hide true_rank from tensor shape; padded_rank itself remains visible to the GPU.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our rank padding hides the LoRA rank.

claims.claim: Loss / optimizer is fully outsourced to the GPU.
claims.paper_safe_wording: Loss + optimizer remain trusted-side; only forward / backward matmuls cross the boundary.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Loss / optimizer run on the untrusted GPU.

claims.claim: Protection against a compromised TEE.
claims.paper_safe_wording: We assume the TEE is honest; a compromised TEE breaks every guarantee we evaluate.
claims.unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Our scheme protects against a compromised TEE.
